commit.message:
fix: Telegram "no response generated" — synthesis, person fields, calendar range (#44)

Three root causes for empty/wrong Telegram responses:

1. When the LLM exhausted all 5 tool rounds, the synthesis round ran
   without tools AND without any instruction to produce text. The LLM
   saw a conversation ending in tool results and produced nothing.
   Fix: add explicit synthesis instruction + fallback from tool results.

2. person_info omitted birthday, company, and position fields — data
   from Apple Contacts was fetched but never shown to the LLM.

3. Calendar search hardcoded ±30 days, missing recurring events like
   birthdays months away. Widened default to ±180 days.

Also: gitignore live e2e test file (contains real names for integration
tests against live server).

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix npm detection: use exit code instead of output string

The previous logic checked if "npm" appeared in output, but error messages
like "which: npm: Command not found" also contain "npm", causing false
positives. Now uses the exit code from `which` directly.

commit.message:
feat: HL7 validation engine with UI warning display (#46) (#51)

- Add `src/validation.rs`: rule-based validator covering MSH required
  fields (MSH-3,4,9,10,11,12) universally, plus message-type–specific
  rules for ADT (EVN/PID/PV1 + PID-3/5/8), ORU^R01 (OBR/OBX + key
  fields), ORM^O01, OML^O21, SIU, and MDM families.
- Extend `Hl7Message` with `validation_warnings: Vec<ValidationWarning>`
  and `Hl7MessageSummary` with `validation_warning_count: usize`.
- Wire validator as the third parser pass (after dictionary injection).
- UI — list row: amber `⚠ N` badge next to the message type when
  warnings are present.
- UI — detail Segments tab: collapsible warning panel listing each
  warning with segment/field reference and human-readable message.

4 new tests (valid ADT has no warnings, missing PID-3, missing PV1,
unknown type produces no spurious warnings).

Closes #46

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix search, embeddings, and CI/CD secret retention

- Fix indexer to populate embedding_vec (pgvector) in addition to JSONB
  embedding column so semantic search can use records after insert
- Add migration 0014 to backfill embedding_vec for all existing records
  missing it (records inserted after migration 0005 had NULL embedding_vec)
- Add Alembic merge migrations (0012, 0013) to resolve multi-head conflict
  after StatFin/Eurostat/OECD/WorldBank URL fixes
- Standardize WorldBank portal_id to 'worldbank' in adapter and harvest job
- Add 'worldbank' and 'undata' aliases to orchestrator _get_adapter
- Fix deploy-app workflow: restore required secrets and set all secret-ref
  env vars (TDO_API_KEYS, EMBEDDING_ENDPOINT, EMBEDDING_API_KEY) on every
  deploy so CI/CD no longer wipes them when creating new revisions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Polish edit page UI: accessibility, microcopy, and UX psychology

Accessibility:
- aria-label on all icon-only buttons (bubble, close, send, reset)
- role="dialog" + aria-label on panel
- role="alert" on error messages
- role="status" + aria-live="polite" on loading overlay
- aria-expanded/aria-haspopup on model picker
- role="listbox" + role="option" + aria-selected on model dropdown
- Visible focus rings (focus:ring-2) on all interactive elements
- sr-only label on textarea
- aria-label on API key input
- role="separator" on dividers

Microcopy:
- "Undo all" → "Reset" (clearer consequence)
- "Describe a specific change..." → example-based placeholder
- Removed filler "Press Enter to send" instruction
- "Or focus on one area" → "Pick a specific rule" (action-oriented)
- "Add API key (optional)" → "Use your own API key" + explanation
- "Remove API key" → "Disconnect API key" (specific verb)
- Error messages now have dismiss button
- Better progress text ("Reading the page..." not "Working on it...")

UX Psychology:
- Default model changed to Gemini (free, works without API key — default bias)
- Check icon on selected model (confirmation feedback)
- Check icon on "edited" badge (success signal)
- Show more button uses "X more rules" not just "X more"
- All text bumped from 10px to 11px minimum for readability
- Icon sizes bumped from 13px to 14px for better touch targets
- Send button enlarged from 24px to 28px (Fitts's Law)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(gsm): double-click on travel cards to filter Records

Add dblclick event handlers on border crossing and overnight stay
cards that filter the Records table by the relevant date range and
scroll to it — same UX pattern as the heatmap cell click filter.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add /dev session scratchpad and in-session continuation

Session scratchpad (DEV_SESSION.md, gitignored):
  /dev session: <notes>  — set working context for the session
  /dev session show      — view current notes
  /dev session clear     — wipe notes
  All /dev planning and implementation prompts automatically prepend
  session notes so Claude Code has goal/direction without re-explanation.

In-session continuation:
  After a confirmed implementation the task stays alive in a new
  `awaiting_followup` state. The user sends the next instruction directly
  (no /dev prefix) and the full history of completed steps is included in
  each subsequent Claude Code prompt. Send `done` to end the session.
  On discard or error mid-session, the session history is preserved and
  the task returns to awaiting_followup rather than terminating.

Also refactored shared logic into startIteration() and buildPlanPrompt() /
buildImplPrompt() helpers to keep the planning and implementation prompts
consistent across first-run and continuation paths.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix html field to return cleaned HTML instead of raw HTML (#4)

The `html` field in the extractContent response was returning the same
raw page HTML as `rawHtml`, making both fields identical. Per the
Firecrawl API convention, `html` should return the cleaned HTML content
(with script, style, nav, header, and footer elements removed), while
`rawHtml` returns the unprocessed page HTML.

This fix uses the already-computed `content` variable (which has those
elements stripped) for the `html` field.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(v0.3.22): corregir carga definitiva de widgets en BentoGrid

Causa raíz del bug (widgets invisibles en primera carga):
1. `projectsHydrated` bloqueaba el render completo de BentoGrid —
   el spinner de "cargando" usaba `text-muted-foreground` (invisible
   en temas oscuros = pantalla negra) hasta que Zustand persist
   terminaba su rehidratación asíncrona.
2. `useLayoutEffect` de medición de `containerWidth` también estaba
   bloqueado por `projectsHydrated` → `containerWidth` permanecía 0
   → `<Responsive>` nunca se renderizaba aunque los widgets ya estuvieran.
3. `WidgetEmptyState` se mostraba antes de que `initWidgets` completara,
   dando la impresión de que no había widgets cuando sí los había.

Fix:
- Early return solo depende de `!mounted` (sin `projectsHydrated`)
- `useLayoutEffect` mide con deps `[mounted, widgetsInitialized]`:
  cuando los widgets pasan de vacíos→cargados, el div con
  `containerDivRef` aparece en el DOM y la re-ejecución del
  effect mide el ancho correctamente
- Antes de mostrar `WidgetEmptyState` se verifica `widgetsInitialized`;
  si aún está cargando, muestra spinner en lugar del estado vacío
- El sync de `activeProjectId` → widget store sigue esperando
  `projectsHydrated` (comportamiento correcto para multi-workspace)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix sourceUrl casing to match OpenAPI schema and Firecrawl API (#3)

The metadata field was returned as `sourceUrl` but the OpenAPI response
schema defines it as `sourceURL`, matching the Firecrawl API convention.
This casing mismatch caused SDK clients to receive `undefined` when
accessing `metadata.sourceURL`.

fix: Disable emitDecoratorMetadata to resolve circular dependency crash

Identifier and Reference have a circular import. emitDecoratorMetadata
generates eager __metadata("design:type", Reference) calls that fail
before the module is initialized. Neither class-validator nor
class-transformer depend on design:type metadata.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(btc-cioh-binary): delete-on-spend UTXO — borne LMDB utxo_db à ~6 GB

Sans suppression, utxo_db grossissait indéfiniment (50M entrées à 7%
de la chaîne → crash MDB_MAP_FULL à blk 400/5432).
Fix : txn.delete(k) après résolution de chaque input dépensé.
UTXO set courant BTC ≈ 90M × 71 bytes ≈ 6 GB → mapsize réduit à 15 GB.

Fix browser resource leak by closing browser after search (#2)

The browser instance launched in WebSearch.handle() was never closed
after use, which could lead to resource exhaustion. Wrap the search
and content extraction logic in a try/finally block to ensure
browser.close() is always called.
